name: Triple Pear Puree
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and core 6 lb Anjou pears, cutting each pear into 1/8 ths.
2. In a heavy large saucepan, combine the pear pieces with 1/2 cup water.
3. Add 2/3 cup pear vinegar and 2/3 cup sugar to the pan.
4. Gently add 2/3 cup of Poire William or pear brandy to the mixture.
5. Simmer the pears and water, stirring occasionally, until the pears are very soft, about 40 minutes.
6. Puree the mixture in batches using a blender until smooth.
7. Return the pureed mixture to the saucepan.
8. Stir in the remaining vinegar and sugar.
9. Continue simmering until the mixture is reduced by 1/3 and thickened to the consistency of applesauce, stirring frequently for about 40 minutes.
10. Mix in the Poire William or pear brandy.
11. Allow the Triple Pear Puree to cool to room temperature before serving.
12. The puree can be prepared up to 2 days in advance and stored refrigerated.
(Note: Recipe By Terry Pogue, tpogue@idsonline.com)
